title: D-Wave, Anduril, Davidson to Collaborate on Quantum Applications for Defense - NAM
why:
- D-Wave, Anduril Industries, and Davidson Technologies are collaborating to develop quantum-classical hybrid solutions for U.S. air and missile defense problems.
- In their initial proof of concept, the companies compared classical computing with D-Wave’s Stride hybrid solver, which integrates quantum and classical computing.
- The D-Wave Stride hybrid solver was at least 10 times faster than classical computing, mitigated threats more effectively by 9–12%, and intercepted an additional 45–60 missiles out of 500 in attack simulations.
- The collaboration is a significant step in applying quantum computing to mission-critical, real-world defense applications.
- The companies plan to expand their partnership into other domains, including scalable and distributed manufacturing and cyber defense.
